mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment rates signal deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce corporate profit expectations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in GDP and industrial production signals robust economic demand, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days through stronger corporate earnings expectations.
failure_condition: The joint effect of GDPC1 and INDPRO fails to explain more than 15% of SPX return variance in 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in inflation (CPI) and federal funds rates signal a monetary tightening cycle that raises equity discount rates, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 50% of instances where both CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS exceed thresholds.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production signals improved supply-side capacity utilization, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days through stronger manufacturing sector performance.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no significant correlation with INDPRO changes (Pearson r < 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: Declining housing starts signal weak demand for construction, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days through reduced sectoral investment and consumer confidence.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 50% of triggered instances despite negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth combined with low interest rates signals a tight labor market and accommodative monetary policy, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction between PAYEMS and FEDFUNDS fails to explain more than 10% of SPX return variance in 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in GDP and retail sales signals strong consumer demand, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days through elevated corporate revenue expectations.
failure_condition: The joint effect of GDPC1 and RSAFS fails to explain more than 20% of SPX return variance in 3-year rolling windows.
